title: relay_event_loop exits on ClientEvent::Disconnected but not on channel closed — two different exit paths with different semantics, no comment
description: daemon/src/relay.rs relay_event_loop: the None arm (channel closed) emits ConnectionStateChanged{status:'disconnected'} and returns. The ClientEvent::Disconnected arm breaks out of the loop (also causing the function to return, since there is no code after the loop). These two paths have identical visible behavior but different semantic causes: one is a clean shutdown (channel closed by the connector), the other is an explicit disconnect signal. The distinction is important for debugging. A contributor reading the code and seeing the break in the Disconnected arm will not immediately understand that this exits the function — there is no code after the match loop. Suggest: replace break with return, and add comments distinguishing the two shutdown paths.
